title: Statement To Ledger Mapping Review
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: statement to ledger mapping review.
assumptions: Statement lines are uniquely identifiable and can be allocated to a single active case.
Chart-of-accounts codes (COA) and mapping rules are authoritative for automated suggestions.
Approvers and reviewers referenced exist and are reachable through the back-office workflow.
Supporting documents, when required, are available and can be associated with the case before final validation.
Initial object inventory provides representative candidate mappings for workflow execution.
risks: Misclassification of statement lines leading to incorrect ledger posting and audit exceptions.
Missing or incomplete supporting documentation delaying validation and closure.
System integration latency or synchronization issues causing stale candidate lists.
Escalation bottlenecks causing prolonged open cases and audit non‑compliance.
Overreliance on automated rule suggestions without adequate review for edge cases.
